## Main Conference (5)

### [Beyond Isolated Investor: Predicting Startup Success via Roleplay-Based Collective Agents](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%23AI4T79>)

**Catalog ID:** 2026-ijcai-liu-beyond-isolated-investor

**Authors:** Zhongyang Liu, Haoyu Pei, Xiangyi Xiao, Xiaocong Du, Yihui Li, Suting Hong, Kunpeng Zhang, Haipeng Zhang

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2026

**Track / presentation:** Main · Oral

**Subvenue:** Special Track on AI4Tech: AI Enabling Critical Technologies

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-12

**Topics:** Financial Agents · Evaluation

**Assets / frequency:** Not Applicable

**Tasks:** startup financing prediction · venture-capital group-decision simulation

**Focus:** Models venture-capital financing decisions as interactions among heterogeneous investor agents and compares their decisions with observed investor choices.

**Why it matters:** Evaluates collective agents on a real private-market investment-screening decision rather than generic company prediction alone.

**Notes:** The visible official paper ID is #AI4T79 and the official subject tag is AI4Finance. The URL points to the shared accepted-paper roster via a text fragment rather than a standalone paper page. IJCAI also schedules a poster.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%23AI4T79>) · [Paper](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%23AI4T79>) · [Code](<https://github.com/ZhangDataLab/SimVC-CAS>)

### [DiffLOB: Diffusion Models for Counterfactual Generation in Limit Order Books](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%23AI4T199>)

**Catalog ID:** 2026-ijcai-wang-difflob

**Authors:** Zhuohan Wang, Carmine Ventre

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2026

**Track / presentation:** Main · Oral

**Subvenue:** Special Track on AI4Tech: AI Enabling Critical Technologies

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-12

**Topics:** Market Microstructure · Market Simulation · Synthetic Data · Evaluation

**Assets / frequency:** Not specified

**Tasks:** counterfactual limit-order-book generation · market scenario analysis · future market-regime prediction evaluation

**Focus:** Generates regime-conditioned counterfactual limit-order-book trajectories and tests realism, intervention validity, and downstream predictive usefulness.

**Why it matters:** Supports market stress and scenario analysis with controllable synthetic microstructure paths.

**Notes:** The visible official paper ID is #AI4T199 and the official subject tag is AI4Finance. The URL points to the shared accepted-paper roster via a text fragment rather than a standalone paper page. IJCAI also schedules a poster.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%23AI4T199>) · [Paper](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%23AI4T199>)

### [Dual-Adversarial Dynamic Variational Asset Pricing with Adaptive Spatio-Temporal Feature Clustering for Portfolio Recommendation](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%235993>)

**Catalog ID:** 2026-ijcai-fang-dual-adversarial-asset-pricing

**Authors:** Yupeng Fang, Ruirui Liu, Xinyu Xia, Huichou Huang, Johannes Ruf, Qingyao Wu

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2026

**Track / presentation:** Main · Oral

**Subvenue:** Main Track

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-12

**Topics:** Factor Investing · Alpha Modeling · Evaluation

**Assets / frequency:** Equities

**Tasks:** dynamic asset pricing · portfolio recommendation

**Focus:** Learns dynamic risk factors and exposures with variational, adversarial, and spatio-temporal components in a joint asset-pricing and portfolio-recommendation model.

**Why it matters:** Turns nonlinear risk-factor estimation into a directly evaluated stock recommendation decision under noisy returns and changing conditions.

**Notes:** The visible official paper ID is #5993. The URL points to the shared accepted-paper roster via a text fragment rather than a standalone paper page. IJCAI also schedules a poster.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%235993>) · [Paper](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%235993>)

### [TransAlpha: Lightweight Design Empowers Stock Return Forecasting](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%23943>)

**Catalog ID:** 2026-ijcai-yang-transalpha

**Authors:** Xiao Yang

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2026

**Track / presentation:** Main · Oral

**Subvenue:** Main Track

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-12

**Topics:** Financial Forecasting · Alpha Modeling · Evaluation

**Assets / frequency:** Equities · Intraday

**Tasks:** intraday stock-return forecasting · portfolio profitability evaluation

**Focus:** Builds a lightweight cross-sectional Transformer for 15-minute A-share return signals and evaluates predictive power and portfolio profitability across broad equity-index segments.

**Why it matters:** Links intraday return forecasts to portfolio profitability rather than stopping at statistical prediction accuracy.

**Notes:** The visible official paper ID is #943. The URL points to the shared accepted-paper roster via a text fragment rather than a standalone paper page. IJCAI also schedules a poster.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%23943>) · [Paper](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%23943>)

### [Vector-Quantized Discrete Latent Factors Meet Financial Priors: Dynamic Cross-Sectional Stock Ranking Prediction for Portfolio Construction](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%232878>)

**Catalog ID:** 2026-ijcai-kim-prism-vq

**Authors:** Namhyoung Kim, Jae Wook Song

**Venue / year:** IJCAI · 2026

**Track / presentation:** Main · Oral

**Subvenue:** Main Track

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-12

**Topics:** Factor Investing · Alpha Modeling · Evaluation

**Assets / frequency:** Equities

**Tasks:** cross-sectional stock ranking · portfolio construction

**Focus:** Combines prior factors, vector-quantized latent factors, and regime-conditioned experts to rank CSI 300 and S&amp;P 500 stocks and evaluate constructed portfolios.

**Why it matters:** Connects interpretable financial priors and latent market structure to security ranking and portfolio outcomes.

**Notes:** The visible official paper ID is #2878. The URL points to the shared accepted-paper roster via a text fragment rather than a standalone paper page. IJCAI also schedules a poster.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%232878>) · [Paper](<https://2026.ijcai.org/accepted-papers/#:~:text=%232878>) · [Code](<https://github.com/finxlab/PRISM-VQ>)
